channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Gonzaga beats No. 19 Saint Mary's 58-51 to capture West Coast Conference Tournament title
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The metrics — and oddsmakers — indicated Gonzaga was far better than its record, and there was little doubt the Bulldogs had the ability to once again compete at a high level. Maybe it was simply a matter of timing.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Utes knocked out of Big 12 Tournament by UCF
item.description: KANSAS CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was a tumultuous season for the Runnin' Utes, and it most likely came to an end Tuesday night with an 87-72 loss to UCF in the first round of the Big 12 Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Eagles send Gardner-Johnson to Texans. Vikings bolster offensive, defensive lines in NFL free agency
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles' top-ranked defense is getting a major makeover a month after ending Patrick Mahomes' bid to lead the Kansas City Chiefs to a third consecutive Super Bowl title.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pacers' Tyrese Haliburton converts last-second 'football play' into 4 points to stun Bucks
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Tyrese Haliburton described it as “a football play” — and he converted the extra point, too.
item.dc:creator: MARK AMBROGI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No Donovan Mitchell? No problem for Cavaliers as they tie their team record with 15th straight win
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — The wins and accomplishments keep piling up for the NBA-leading Cleveland Cavaliers. They’ve been so frequent that players are having a hard time keeping track. “What did we do? Oh, we clinched the Central Division,” center Jarrett Allen said, chuckling. “That’s something.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN DULIK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Junior Bridgeman, businessman and basketball standout for Louisville and Milwaukee Bucks, dies at 71
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Junior Bridgeman, a basketball standout who led Louisville to a Final Four, starred for the NBA's Milwaukee Bucks and then launched an even more successful career as a businessman with stakes in restaurants, publishing and the Bucks franchise, died Tuesday. He was 71.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Two-time Super Bowl champion Justin Reid lands with Saints: reports
item.description: Justin Reid appears to have found a new home for next season. According to multiple reports, the star defensive back and the Saints reached an agreement on a three-year contract.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NBA-leading Cavaliers beat Nets 109-104 for their second 15-game win streak of season
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — All-Star Darius Garland scored 18 of his 30 points in the fourth quarter and the NBA-leading Cleveland Cavaliers rallied for a 109-104 victory over the Brooklyn Nets on Tuesday night, matching the longest winning streak in franchise history at 15 games.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN DULIK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Junior Bridgeman, ex-NBA star and billionaire businessman, dead at 71
item.description: Junior Bridgeman, ex-NBA star who became a billionaire part-owner of the Milwaukee Bucks, died at the age of 71 years old during a luncheon where he said he was having a heart attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PSG ousts Liverpool from Champions League on penalties to join Barcelona, Bayern and Inter in QF
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Gianluigi Donnarumma came through and Paris Saint-Germain defeated Liverpool in a penalty shootout to join Barcelona, Bayern Munich and Inter Milan in the quarterfinals of the Champions League on Tuesday. PSG beat Liverpool 1-0 on the night to level the aggregate score at 1-1.
item.dc:creator: TALES AZZONI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Former NFL player David Pollack asks for prayers after revealing wife will undergo surgery for brain cancer
item.description: Ex-NFL player David Pollack asked for prayers on social media this week after sharing that his wife, who was diagnosed with brain cancer, will undergo surgery on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Several once-highly ranked teams enter conference tournament week in need of wins to make NCAA field
item.description: LAWRENCE, Kan. (AP) — This was a time early in the season when Kansas seemed to be an insurmountable juggernaut, full of high-profile transfers such as Zeke Mayo and AJ Storr, All-American candidates such as Hunter Dickinson and veterans accustomed to winning. They were the preseason No.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: QB Sam Darnold agrees on a 3-year, $100.5 million deal with Seahawks, AP source says
item.description: The Seattle Seahawks found the replacement for Geno Smith, agreeing to a three-year, $100.5 million contract with Sam Darnold, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Report: Bears sign DE Dayo Odeyingbo to 3-year deal
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — The Bears have signed defensive end Dayo Odeyingbo to a three-year deal, according to NFL insider Ian Rapoport. The 25-year-old was a second-round pick by the Colts in 2021 and addresses a need the team had on the defensive line.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Boys Soccer CIF Division IV SoCal State Regional Championship: Pacific Ridge 3, Central Union 2
item.description: The CIF Division IV Boys Soccer SoCal Regional State Championship featured two San Diego Section teams, as the Pacific Ridge Firebirds hosted the Central Union Spartans.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Giddey scores 26 to lead Bulls' comeback from 17 down to beat the Heat 114-109
item.description: Josh Giddey scored 26 points and the Chicago Bulls rallied from a 17-point, first-half deficit to beat the Miami Heat 114-109 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tatum scores 40, Celtics beat Lakers 111-101 after James leaves late with groin injury
item.description: Jayson Tatum had 40 points, 12 rebounds, and eight assists, and the Boston Celtics held off a late rally to beat the Los Angeles Lakers 111-101 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amber Whiting resigns as BYU women's basketball coach
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU women's basketball coach Amber Whiting announced she is resigning after three seasons in Provo. The decision to part ways was mutual between Whiting and the school. Assistant coach Lee Cummard will serve as the interim head coach for now.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Blackhawks in action against the Predators following overtime victory
item.description: Chicago Blackhawks (20-35-8, in the Central Division) vs. Nashville Predators (23-32-7, in the Central Division) Nashville, Tennessee; Saturday, 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boys Volleyball: Cathedral Catholic 3, Granite Hills 0
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Boys Volleyball action on Wednesday night Cathedral Catholic beat Granite Hills in three sets.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5
